name: Blynk IoT Platform API
what_it_does: IoT platform with REST API — enabling hardware makers and IoT developers to connect Arduino, ESP32, Raspberry Pi, and other microcontrollers to the cloud, build mobile dashboards without coding, manage device fleets, send notifications, collect sensor data, and control devices remotely via HTTP API or native device libraries.
use_cases: Controlling IoT device GPIO pins and outputs via Blynk HTTP API for remote device management
Reading sensor data from Blynk virtual pins via API for IoT data collection
Managing Blynk device fleet and provisioning via API for mass IoT deployment
Triggering Blynk notifications and alerts via API for IoT event management
Building Blynk automations and schedules via API for IoT workflow orchestration
not_for: Industrial IoT requiring certified protocols (IEC 62443, MQTT broker control)
Teams needing on-premises self-hosted IoT platform (use ThingsBoard OSS)
High-frequency (>1000/sec) sensor data streams (Blynk is consumer/prosumer scale)
best_when: A hardware maker, maker community member, or small business needs a quick way to connect ESP32/Arduino to cloud with a mobile dashboard, notifications, and REST API — without building IoT infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need industrial-grade IoT protocols, on-premises deployment, or high-frequency sensor data at scale.

auth.notes: Device Auth Token for device API calls. User API token for user-level management API. BlynkAuthToken header or URL parameter. Per-device tokens (each device has unique token). Organization API tokens for fleet management. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhook (HTTP trigger) for device events. Native device libraries (Arduino, MicroPython, Node.js, C++).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: 2 devices, basic templates, community support
pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2013 by Pavel Shardin and Dmitriy Dumanskiy. Raised $1.7M. Originally open-source Blynk Server (legacy), now Blynk.Cloud SaaS (v1.0, 2023 complete rewrite). 500,000+ developers globally. Very popular in maker/Arduino community. Native libraries for 50+ hardware platforms. Mobile app (iOS, Android) for no-code device dashboards. White-label IoT product builder for businesses. Per-device pricing model. Used by 30+ countries, maker community, small businesses, education.

performance.rate_limits: 1 request per second per device (free); higher on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST HTTP API for device and management. Per-device and user API tokens. Webhook for device events. SDKs for Arduino/embedded. Good documentation. GDPR compliant. 1 req/sec rate limit per device on free. Per-device pricing model. Legacy Blynk Server (v0.1) vs Blynk.Cloud (v1.0) are different — use v1.0 API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device pin reads idempotent. Device state reads idempotent. Pin writes are state-setting (idempotent for same value). Event triggers not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Legacy Blynk Server (v0.1) vs Blynk.Cloud (v1.0) have DIFFERENT APIs — use v1.0
Per-device auth token — agents managing fleet need token per device
1 request/second rate limit per device on free plan — queue requests
Mobile app is primary UX — API is secondary to native device libraries
Organization API vs device API are separate auth patterns

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced for HTTP API. GDPR compliant. Per-device auth tokens provide device isolation. User API tokens for management. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Device tokens in firmware — secure storage required. No IP whitelisting documented. US and EU data residency. GDPR-aware data handling. Device auth tokens must be protected in firmware (flash storage vulnerabilities).
